events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the assessment directly to the required JSON file. This is a code/compliance judgment, so I’m treating the reversed primary/secondary terminations as a failed installed condition rather than a measurement that can be accepted with tolerance.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.92,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Transformer primary and secondary conductors are landed on the wrong terminals.\",
    \"Reversed terminations can place incorrect voltage on equipment, defeat intended overcurrent protection coordination, and create shock, arc-flash, and equipment-damage hazards.\",
    \"Installation is not acceptable for energization until terminations, grounding, bonding, nameplate ratings, and derived-system connections are corrected and verified.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not energize the transformer.\",
    \"Lock out and tag out the affected circuit if any source is available.\",
    \"Re-terminate primary and secondary conductors per engineered drawings, nameplate, and manufacturer wiring diagram.\",
    \"Verify separately derived system grounding and bonding requirements before inspection and testing.\",
    \"Perform continuity, insulation resistance, phase, polarity, voltage, and torque verification before release.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Primary/secondary landed reversed is an installed-defective and non-compliant transformer connection, not a tolerable dimensional variance. At rough-complete the equipment may be physically installed, but the electrical configuration is unsafe and fails because it does not match the intended primary/secondary ratings, wiring diagram, grounding arrangement, or engineered design. NEC Article 450 governs transformer installation and protection, and NEC 250.30 applies to grounding and bonding of separately derived systems where applicable.\",
  \"references\": [\"NEC Article 450\", \"NEC 250.30\", \"project engineered drawings\", \"manufacturer transformer nameplate and wiring diagram\"]
}' > /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
